tool_name: Digital Twin Prototype ROI vs Physical Build — Cost Avoidance, TTM & NPV (ISO 23247)

inputs.name: Physical Prototype Unit Cost
inputs.hint: Full cost: materials + machining + tooling + assembly + testing per build

inputs.name: Average Physical Iteration Count (baseline)
inputs.hint: Historical average design cycles before release — typical product development: 3–8

inputs.name: Expected Digital Iterations Before First Physical Build
inputs.hint: Virtual design cycles replacing physical builds — typically 5–15 per 1 physical

inputs.name: Remaining Physical Builds Required After DT Validation
inputs.hint: Even with DT, at least 1 physical validation build is typically required

inputs.name: Field / Validation Test Cost (per programme)
inputs.hint: Environmental, durability, regulatory testing costs per full programme

inputs.name: Field Test Cost Reduction from DT Preflight
inputs.hint: DT-validated designs require fewer field test cycles — typical: 20–50% reduction

inputs.name: Digital Twin Platform License Cost
inputs.hint: Ansys Twin Builder / Siemens Simcenter / MATLAB SimScape / PTC ThingWorx annual fee

inputs.name: HPC / Cloud Simulation Compute Cost
inputs.hint: AWS/Azure HPC instances for FEA, CFD, MBD simulations — per product programme

inputs.name: IoT Sensor & Data Infrastructure CAPEX
inputs.hint: Sensors, edge devices, connectivity, historian — one-time or amortised per programme

inputs.name: DT Model Engineering & Calibration Effort
inputs.hint: Engineer hours for model build, parameter identification, and validation

inputs.name: Time-to-Market Acceleration (days gained)
inputs.hint: Calendar days saved by parallel virtual validation vs sequential physical build

inputs.name: Daily Market Revenue at Launch
inputs.hint: Expected daily revenue from early market entry — opportunity value per day of acceleration

inputs.name: Expected Warranty / Field Failure Reduction
inputs.hint: DT-validated designs typically show 15–35% reduction in first-year warranty claims

formulas: Physical_Proto_Cost_Baseline_USD = physical_proto_unit_cost_usd * physical_iterations_avg + field_test_cost_usd   // [USD] Historical physical programme cost
Physical_Proto_Cost_With_DT_USD = physical_proto_unit_cost_usd * physical_iterations_with_dt + field_test_cost_usd * (1 - field_test_reduction_pct / 100)   // [USD] Physical cost after DT validation
DT_Engineering_Cost_USD = engineering_effort_hrs * engineer_rate_usd_hr   // [USD] Model build labour cost
DT_Total_Investment_USD = dt_software_license_usd_yr + hpc_compute_cost_usd + iot_sensor_capex_usd + DT_Engineering_Cost_USD   // [USD] Total DT programme investment
Prototype_Cost_Avoidance_USD = Physical_Proto_Cost_Baseline_USD - Physical_Proto_Cost_With_DT_USD   // [USD] Direct savings vs baseline
TTM_Revenue_Gain_USD = ttm_gain_days * daily_market_revenue_usd   // [USD] Revenue from early market entry
Annual_Benefit_USD = Prototype_Cost_Avoidance_USD + TTM_Revenue_Gain_USD + Warranty_Saving_USD   // [USD] Total year-1 benefit (cost avoidance + revenue + warranty)
Recurring_Annual_Benefit_USD = Warranty_Saving_USD   // [USD/year] Ongoing annual benefit (warranty only)
Annuity_Factor = (1 - POWER(1 + wacc_pct / 100, -programme_life_years)) / (wacc_pct / 100)   // [—] PV annuity factor
NPV_USD = Prototype_Cost_Avoidance_USD + TTM_Revenue_Gain_USD + Warranty_Saving_USD * Annuity_Factor - DT_Total_Investment_USD   // [USD] Net Present Value
Simple_Payback_Months = DT_Total_Investment_USD > 0 && Annual_Benefit_USD > 0 ? (DT_Total_Investment_USD / Annual_Benefit_USD) * 12 : 999   // [months] Simple payback
Breakeven_Proto_Reduction = DT_Total_Investment_USD / physical_proto_unit_cost_usd   // [units] Physical builds avoided needed to break even

dt_iterations_vs_physical.error_msg: Physical builds after DT must be less than baseline. If DT reduces zero physical iterations, there is no prototype cost avoidance benefit.

engineer_rate_minimum.error_msg: Engineer rate cannot be below $10/h. Use fully burdened rate including overhead, benefits, and indirect costs.

programme_life_range.error_msg: Programme life must be 1–15 years for meaningful NPV analysis.

smart_warnings.message: NEGATIVE NPV: Digital Twin investment does not recover its cost at this discount rate and programme scale. Review: (1) Increase programme reuse across multiple products, (2) Negotiate platform licence sharing, (3) Quantify previously excluded benefits (regulatory approval acceleration, recall avoidance).

Engineering labour >60% of DT investment: Model build effort is dominant cost. Evaluate: (1) Reuse existing simulation models, (2) Use vendor-supplied component models, (3) Phased modelling — start with highest-risk subsystems only.

Zero TTM gain entered: Time-to-market acceleration is typically the highest-value DT benefit. If parallel virtual/physical development is not planned, significant ROI is being excluded. Replan development timeline to capture TTM benefit.

Payback >24 months: Above typical threshold for product development tool investment. Consider: (1) Shared licence across multiple simultaneous programmes, (2) Phased IoT sensor rollout, (3) Open-source simulation platforms to reduce licence cost.

smart_warnings.source: Digital Twin Best Practice — McKinsey / Deloitte
smart_warnings.message: Low virtual iteration count (<5): DT value scales with simulation depth. Less than 5 virtual iterations suggests the model is not being used to its potential. Target 10–20 virtual design cycles per physical build for full benefit realisation.

IoT CAPEX exceeds 50% of prototype cost baseline: Sensor investment may be disproportionate. Evaluate: (1) Virtual sensor placement using FEA instead of physical measurement, (2) Shared sensor infrastructure across production lines, (3) Edge AI for data reduction before cloud transmission.
